results.title: Manhattan Community Boathouse - Free Kayaking in NYC
results.content: # **Manhattan Community Boathouse offers free kayaking at Pier 96 in Hudson River Park**. * We supply everything you need: A life jacket, a paddle, a single or two-person kayak, and a locker for anything you’d like to keep dry. You may not get a chance to kayak if you arrive less than an hour before we close. * Participants must be at least 18 years old and know how to swim. * Participants under the age of 18 must be accompanied by a parent or legal guardian, and minors over 50 pounds must know how to swim. * An adult must be present with minors at all times, and children may not be left on the pier unsupervised. Completing a waiver in advance will save time when you arrive at the boathouse, but it is not a reservation. You may still need to wait in line to check in when you arrive, or for a kayak to become available.

results.title: New York City Water Trail : New York City Department of Parks & Recreation : NYC Parks
results.content: # New York City Water Trail : New York City Department of Parks & Recreation : NYC Parks. NYC Resources311Office of the MayorImage 1: The Official New York City Website. *   Image 6: Follow on LinkedIn. Official Website of the New York City Department of Parks & Recreation. # New York City Water Trail. New York City is rich in water. The New York City Water Trail connects 160 square miles of rivers, bays, creeks, inlets and ocean in the five boroughs suitable for kayaks, canoes, and openwater rowing craft. ### Water Trail Launch Application and Recommendations and Rules. If you have your own kayak, canoe or boat, read up on these rules and suggestions and apply for your permit before heading out on the water trail. ### Riverside Park (79th Street). The kayak launch at Riverside Park (79th Street) is closed for repairs until 2028. The kayak launch at Riverside Park (72nd Street) is closed indefinitely for repairs.

results.title: Urban Kayaking Adventure: 8 Miles on the Bronx River in NYC
results.content:
Urban Kayaking Adventure: 8 Miles on the Bronx River in NYC
Two Feet Outdoors 
55000 subscribers
10658 likes
470230 views
20 Apr 2024
Join me as I kayak down the Bronx River, exploring the hidden gems of nature in the heart of New York City. I paddled the Bronx River Blueway through the Bronx River Forest, the Bronx Zoo, and the New York Botanical Garden. Tackled some challenges along the 8 mile journey, but experienced the rarely seen beauty of the Bronx. Don't forget to subscribe for more epic adventures!

Shout out to the @BronxRiverAlliance for working hard to keep this waterway clean!

results.title: Kayaking - Brooklyn Bridge Park - Brooklyn Bridge Park
results.content: Kayaking in Brooklyn Bridge Park. **Brooklyn Bridge Park** and **Brooklyn Bridge Park Boathouse**partner to offer free kayaking opportunities each summer! The Brooklyn Bridge is seen in the background. View of the Brooklyn Bridge from the Pier 2 launch dock. Family kayaking in Brooklyn Bridge Park. ## Explore the East River Estuary and glide across the water at public kayaking in Brooklyn Bridge Park. Groups of people kayaking by Pier 2 at sunset. Groups kayaking at sunset with Pier 1 Salt Marsh and the Brooklyn Bridge in the background. Family kayaking on a sunny day with the Brooklyn Bridge in the background. Join Brooklyn Bridge Park and Brooklyn Bridge Park Boathouse for free public kayaking! Free public kayaking run by Brooklyn Bridge Park in partnership with Brooklyn Bridge Park Boathouse is run at the Pier 2 dock. **Yes, kayaking in Brooklyn Bridge Park is completely free.** The Brooklyn Bridge Park Boathouse runs on the generosity of volunteers and your donations.

results.title: How to get into kayaking in NYC?? - Reddit
results.content: Pier 26, Pier 40, Gansevoort Peninsula, Pier 84 and Pier 96 are all public launch sites within Hudson River Park that don't require a permit.
